description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a dark wooden exterior and a large, convex glass screen dominating the front face. The television features a pair of long, thin metal antennas extending upward from a white base on the top surface. The front panel includes a row of small, round control buttons or dials beneath the screen, likely for channel and volume adjustment. The sides and back are solid wood with a rich, brown grain, giving the television a classic, retro appearance. The base is slightly protruding and appears to be made of a lighter-colored material, possibly plastic or metal. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-20th-century television sets.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a dark wooden exterior, convex glass screen, and metal antennas. Features include a row of control buttons beneath the screen and a prominent wood grain finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, row of control buttons, and both antennas extending upward.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid wooden panel and the white antenna base on top, with no screen or buttons visible.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the uninterrupted wooden back panel and the base of the antennas, with no controls or screen visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same solid wooden panel and antenna base as the other side, with no screen or buttons visible.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, dark brown wooden bezel with a glossy finish and rounded edges
attribute_control_button_layout: row of buttons beneath the screen on the front panel
